test_description: Compare direct memorization from the scholar-book proof witness against direct memorization from the authoritative fetched proof layer.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the system is wrongly treating witness text as the final memorization source.
why_this_test_matters: This is the core trust distinction in the case.

test_description: Compare one pair of proof variants with only non-significant wording drift against one pair where the wording materially changes the conclusion.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Whether the system can distinguish harmless variation from significant variation.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly wants significance-sensitive comparison rather than flat variant handling.

test_description: Test whether the system can correctly link each scholar’s explanation or conclusion to the specific proof version relied on in the book witness layer.
why_this_test_matters: This mapping is a core source of unprecedented analytical value.

test_description: Apply study-friendly chunking directly to the scholar-book witness text and see whether the layer boundary is lost.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Chunking being applied to the wrong layer as if it were harmless formatting.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly rejects mutating the source witness for memorization convenience.

test_description: Introduce a new scholar methodology in which rule, proof, and explanation are interwoven differently from known patterns.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Hard-rule overconfidence and missing uncertainty escalation.
why_this_test_matters: The owner explicitly fears unseen methodologies and unseen edge cases.

test_description: Give the system a variant difference whose significance is genuinely unclear and test whether it reports uncertainty instead of false confidence.
why_this_test_matters: The owner wants uncertainty gates where certainty is not deserved.

test_description: Present a generated summary or help layer without clear separation from the original source text.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Secondary help silently replacing or contaminating the source layer.
why_this_test_matters: The owner wants help, but only in parallel layers.

test_description: Remove the authoritative fetched proof layer and see whether the remaining system still pushes direct memorization from the scholar-book witness.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Dependency on loose witness text without authoritative confirmation.
why_this_test_matters: This is a direct failure mode the owner is trying to avoid.

test_description: Run large-scale cross-source analysis and check whether the system can produce scholar/proof/opinion insights without collapsing different proof layers together.
what_it_is_trying_to_expose: Analytics that are impressive but structurally unsafe because the layers were not kept distinct.
why_this_test_matters: The owner wants analysis at scale, but not at the cost of integrity.
